title: Number of 1 Bits
description: Given a non-negative integer `n`, return the number of `1` bits in its binary representation (also known as the Hamming weight).

examples.explanation: 11 in binary is 1011 — three set bits.

hints: Right shift karte ja aur n & 1 check karta ja — har 1 ko count.
Brian Kernighan trick: n = n & (n-1) lowest set bit ko clear karta hai.
Loop tab tak chal jab tak n != 0; iterations = number of set bits.

title: Counting Bits
description: Given a non-negative integer `n`, return an array `ans` of length `n + 1` where, for each `i` in the range `0 <= i <= n`, `ans[i]` is the number of `1` bits in the binary representation of `i`.
hints: Naive: har i ke liye Hamming weight nikaal — O(n log n).
DP trick: ans[i] = ans[i >> 1] + (i & 1).
Ya: ans[i] = ans[i & (i-1)] + 1 — Kernighan recurrence.

title: Reverse Bits
description: Given a 32-bit unsigned integer `n`, return the value obtained by reversing the order of its 32 bits (bit 0 swaps with bit 31, bit 1 with bit 30, and so on).
hints: 32 iterations: result <<= 1; result |= (n & 1); n >>= 1.
JavaScript me result ko unsigned banane ke liye `>>> 0` use kar.
Edge case: n = 0 should return 0; n with only msb set returns 1.

title: Single Number
description: Given a non-empty integer array `riders` in which every element appears exactly twice except for one element that appears only once, return that single element. You must solve it in linear time and use only constant extra space.
constraints: Exactly one element appears once; all others twice
hints: XOR identity: x ^ x = 0, x ^ 0 = x.
Saare elements ka XOR le — duplicates cancel.
Order pe nahi depend karta — XOR commutative aur associative hai.

title: Power of Two
description: Given an integer `n`, return `true` if `n` is a power of two, and `false` otherwise. An integer is a power of two if it equals `2^x` for some non-negative integer `x`. Solve it using bit manipulation, without loops or `Math.log`.
hints: Power of two ka binary me sirf ek hi 1 hota hai.
n > 0 AND (n & (n - 1)) == 0 — yahi check.
Negative aur zero false return karna chahiye.

title: Missing Number
description: Given an array `ids` of length `n` containing `n` distinct integers taken from the range `[0, n]`, exactly one integer in that range is missing from the array. Return the missing integer. Solve it in O(n) time and O(1) extra space using XOR, without relying on summation (to avoid overflow).
hints: XOR 0..n with all array elements — pairs cancel.
Loop me hi index aur value dono XOR kar; final ans = n hi se start kar.
Sum trick bhi correct hai but XOR overflow-proof.

title: Toggle Kth Bit
description: Given a non-negative integer `mask` and a bit index `k` (0-indexed from the least significant bit), toggle bit `k` of `mask` — set it if it is currently `0`, clear it if it is currently `1` — and return the resulting value.
hints: XOR with (1 << k) — set bit toggle ka standard idiom.
AND clears, OR sets, XOR toggles — yaad rakh.
Result negative na ho is liye k <= 30 rakha hai.
editorial:
**Three idioms**:
- Set bit k: `mask | (1 << k)`
- Clear bit k: `mask & ~(1 << k)`
- Toggle bit k: `mask ^ (1 << k)`

```javascript
function togglePermissionBit(mask, k) {
  return mask ^ (1 << k);
}
```

**Time**: O(1). **Space**: O(1).

XOR with a 1 flips the bit (because 0^1=1, 1^1=0). XOR with 0 leaves bits unchanged, so all other positions stay put.

**Pro tip**: To check bit k, use `(mask >> k) & 1` or `(mask & (1 << k)) !== 0`. Easy to confuse 'set' and 'toggle' under interview pressure — XOR = toggle.

title: Sum of Two Integers
description: Given two integers `a` and `b`, return their sum without using the `+` or `-` operators. Use only bitwise operations.
hints: Sum without carry = a XOR b.
Carry = (a AND b) << 1.
Loop until carry becomes 0; sum-without-carry becomes the new a, carry becomes new b.

title: Single Number III
description: Given an integer array `zones` in which exactly two elements appear only once and every other element appears exactly twice, return the two single-occurrence elements as an array sorted in ascending order. Solve it in O(n) time and O(1) extra space.

examples.explanation: 1 and 2 cancel; 3 and 5 are the lonely pair.

constraints: Exactly two values appear once; all others twice
hints: Saare ka XOR = a XOR b (the two unique values).
Lowest set bit of (a XOR b) ek aisa bit hai jo a aur b me alag hai.
Us bit ke basis pe array ko do groups me partition kar — har group me ek unique.

title: Single Number II
description: Given an integer array `redemptions` in which every element appears exactly three times except for one element that appears only once, return that single element. Solve it in O(n) time and O(1) extra space.
constraints: Exactly one element appears once; all others three times
hints: Bitwise count modulo 3 — har bit position ka sum mod 3 lo.
Smarter: do counters `ones`, `twos` as bitmasks tracking 'seen 1 time', 'seen 2 times' mod 3.
Update: ones = (ones XOR x) AND NOT twos; twos = (twos XOR x) AND NOT ones.

title: Bitwise AND of Numbers Range
description: Given two non-negative integers `left` and `right` with `left <= right`, return the bitwise AND of all integers in the inclusive range `[left, right]`.
hints: Looping through every int will TLE for big ranges.
Common prefix of left and right ke baad sab bits eventually 0 ho jaate hain.
Right-shift dono ko jab tak equal nahi ho jaate, count shifts, fir wapas left-shift kar.

title: Subsets
description: Given an array `flags` of distinct integers, return all possible subsets (the power set). Within each subset, elements must appear in their original input order. The subsets must be returned in ascending order of their bitmask index, where subset `i` corresponds to bitmask `i` and bit `b` of the mask being set means `flags[b]` is included.
hints: 2^n subsets — bitmask enumeration ka classic case.
For mask in 0..(1<<n)-1: subset me flags[i] include kar agar (mask>>i)&1.
Output order = mask order itself.

title: XOR Queries of a Subarray
description: Given an integer array `arr` and a list of queries `queries`, where each query is a pair `[l, r]`, return an array in which the value for each query is the bitwise XOR of the elements `arr[l] ^ arr[l+1] ^ ... ^ arr[r]` (inclusive). The result array must have one entry per query, in the same order.
hints: Naive: per-query O(n) — total O(n*q), too slow.
Prefix XOR `p[i]` = arr[0] ^ ... ^ arr[i-1]. Then XOR of [l..r] = p[r+1] ^ p[l].
p[0] = 0; build in single pass.

title: Maximum XOR of Two Numbers in an Array
description: Given an array `nums` of non-negative integers, return the maximum value of `nums[i] XOR nums[j]` over all pairs of indices `i` and `j` with `i != j`. If the array has fewer than two elements, return `0`. Aim for better than O(n^2) time.

examples.explanation: Only one element — XOR with itself is 0.

hints: Greedy bit-by-bit: try to make answer's highest bit 1, fir aage badh.
Bit-trie (binary trie of all numbers) banane se O(32n) ho jata hai.
Edge: single element ⇒ no pair ⇒ return 0.

title: Hamming Distance
description: Given two non-negative integers `a` and `b`, return the minimum number of single-bit flips required to transform `a` into `b`. Each flip toggles exactly one bit, so this equals the number of bit positions at which `a` and `b` differ (their Hamming distance).
hints: Hamming distance dhoondhna hai a aur b ke beech.
a XOR b = positions where they differ.
Popcount of (a XOR b) hi answer hai.
